name: checkvsphere

metadata.name: checkvsphere
classifiers: License :: OSI Approved :: GNU Affero General Public License v3 or later (AGPLv3+)

documentation:
# About

This is a monitoring plugin for naemon, icinga, nagios like systems. It
is meant as a successor of check\_vmware\_esx. check\_vmware\_esx is written
Perl but VMWare™ has dropped support for the Perl SDK. So this plugin is written
in Python using pyVmomi.

## Features

The plugin has modes to check various aspects of these components:

* datastores
* host-runtime
* host-service
* host-storage
* host-nic
* snapshots
* vsan

Check the
[Documentation](https://omd.consol.de/docs/plugins/check_vsphere/)
for further details.

# Installation

```
pip install checkvsphere
```

# LICENSE

If not stated otherwise in a source file everything is licensed under
GNU AFFERO GENERAL PUBLIC LICENSE Version 3.

See also the LICENSE file.

functions.name: checkvsphere.tools.serviceutil.build_full_traversal
documentation:
Builds a traversal spec that will recurse through all objects .. or at
least I think it does. additions welcome.

See com.vmware.apputils.vim25.ServiceUtil.buildFullTraversal in the java
API. Extended by Sebastian Tello's examples from pysphere to reach networks
and datastores.

modules.name: checkvsphere.tools.pchelper
documentation: Property Collector helper module.

functions.name: checkvsphere.tools.pchelper.collect_properties
documentation:
Collect properties for managed objects from a view ref

Check the vSphere API documentation for example on retrieving
object properties:

    - http://goo.gl/erbFDz

Args:
    si          (ServiceInstance): ServiceInstance connection
    view_ref (pyVmomi.vim.view.*): Starting point of inventory navigation
    obj_type      (pyVmomi.vim.*): Type of managed object
    path_set               (list): List of properties to retrieve
    include_mors           (bool): If True include the managed objects
                                   refs in the result

Returns:
    A list of properties for the managed objects
Shamelessly borrowed from:
https://github.com/dnaeon/py-vconnector/blob/master/src/vconnector/core.py

functions.name: checkvsphere.tools.pchelper.get_container_view
documentation:
Get a vSphere Container View reference to all objects of type 'obj_type'

It is up to the caller to take care of destroying the View when no longer
needed.

Args:
    obj_type (list): A list of managed object types

Returns:
    A container view ref to the discovered managed objects

functions.name: checkvsphere.tools.pchelper.search_for_obj
documentation:
Search the managed object for the name and type specified

Sample Usage:

get_obj(content, [vim.Datastore], "Datastore Name")

functions.name: checkvsphere.tools.pchelper.get_all_obj
documentation:
Search the managed object for the name and type specified

Sample Usage:

get_obj(content, [vim.Datastore], "Datastore Name")

functions.name: checkvsphere.tools.pchelper.get_obj
documentation:
Retrieves the managed object for the name and type specified
Throws an exception if of not found.

Sample Usage:

get_obj(content, [vim.Datastore], "Datastore Name")

documentation:
based on:

: <http://code.activestate.com/recipes/577058/>

```
param question

Question to ask

param default

No

True/False
```

classes.name: checkvsphere.tools.cli.Parser

methods.name: checkvsphere.tools.cli.Parser.__init__
documentation:
Defines two arguments groups.
One for the standard arguments and one for sample specific arguments.
The standard group cannot be extended.

methods.name: checkvsphere.tools.cli.Parser.get_args
documentation: Supports the command-line arguments needed to form a connection to vSphere.

methods.name: checkvsphere.tools.cli.Parser._add_sample_specific_arguments
documentation:
Add an argument to the "sample specific arguments" group
Requires a predefined argument from the Argument class.

methods.name: checkvsphere.tools.cli.Parser.add_required_arguments
documentation:
Add a required argument to the "sample specific arguments" group
Requires a predefined argument from the Argument class.

methods.name: checkvsphere.tools.cli.Parser.add_optional_arguments
documentation:
Add an optional argument to the "sample specific arguments" group.
Requires a predefined argument from the Argument class.

methods.name: checkvsphere.tools.cli.Parser.add_custom_argument
documentation:
Uses ArgumentParser.add_argument() to add a full definition of a command line argument
to the "sample specific arguments" group.
https://docs.python.org/3/library/argparse.html#the-add-argument-method

methods.name: checkvsphere.tools.cli.Parser.set_epilog
documentation: Text to display after the argument help

methods.name: checkvsphere.tools.cli.Parser._prompt_for_password
documentation: if no password is specified on the command line, prompt for it

Samples specific argument parser.
Wraps argparse to ease the setup of argument requirements for the samples.

Example:
    parser = cli.Parser()
    parser.add_required_arguments(cli.Argument.VM_NAME)
    parser.add_optional_arguments(cli.Argument.DATACENTER_NAME, cli.Argument.NIC_NAME)
    parser.add_custom_argument(
        '--disk-number', required=True, help='Disk number to change mode.')
    args = parser.get_args()

classes.name: checkvsphere.tools.cli.Argument
documentation:
Predefined arguments to use in the Parser

Example:
    parser = cli.Parser()
    parser.add_optional_arguments(cli.Argument.VM_NAME)
    parser.add_optional_arguments(cli.Argument.DATACENTER_NAME, cli.Argument.NIC_NAME)
